name: num2words (Numbers to Words in Multiple Languages)

agent_readiness.notes: Convert numbers to words in 40+ languages. from num2words import num2words. num2words(42). num2words(42, lang='de'). num2words(42, lang='fr'). num2words(42, to='ordinal'). num2words(42, to='ordinal_num'). num2words(1234.56, lang='en'). num2words(1234.56, to='currency', lang='en', currency='USD'). num2words(0.5, lang='es'). Languages: en, de, fr, es, pt, it, ru, uk, pl, nl, ar, fa, ja, ko, zh, 30+ more. CLI: num2words 42 -l de.

gotchas: LGPL-2.1 — dynamic linking required for commercial use
Currency output format varies by language — test your target locale
Some languages have limited support compared to English
best_when: Multilingual number-to-words for invoices, legal documents
avoid_when: High-volume batch processing (pure Python, not optimized for speed)
summary: num2words converts numbers to words in 40+ languages. num2words(42, lang='de'). ordinal, ordinal_num types. Currency: num2words(1234.56, to='currency', lang='en', currency='USD'). LGPL-2.1 by Savoir-faire Linux. Best for multilingual invoices, legal documents, and currency-in-words. English-only use cases better served by inflect for grammatical control.
